description_long: This object is a large, roughly rectangular stone block with two perpendicular faces forming an L-shape, resembling a corner or boundary segment. The surfaces are uneven and textured, with a natural, weathered stone appearance and subtle color variations in off-white and light gray. The block's faces are thick and solid, with visible roughness and some shallow depressions or tool marks, suggesting it may have been shaped or cut for construction purposes. The edges are not perfectly straight, giving it a slightly irregular, hand-hewn look. There are no visible joints, fasteners, or additional materials, indicating it is a single solid piece. This type of stone block is commonly used as a barrier or boundary in urban or historical architectural settings.
description: A large, L-shaped, rough-textured stone block with off-white and light gray coloration, featuring irregular surfaces and a solid, heavy appearance.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the L-shaped stone block from above, highlighting the inner corner and the rough, pitted surface texture.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the block from a lower angle, emphasizing the depth of the two perpendicular faces and the uneven, natural stone finish.
description_view_2: View 2 displays the block from the opposite side, revealing the thickness of the stone and the continuation of the rough surface.
description_view_3: View 3 shows the block from the back, focusing on the long, flat face and the irregular lower edge.
attribute_location_context: historic urban boundary or park landscaping
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set on compacted earth
attribute_weathering_pattern: The stone shows signs of surface roughness and minor pitting, likely from prolonged exposure to the elements. There are no major chips or cracks, but the texture suggests some natural weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous wall or boundary
